assertion_results.text: JQL queries use the project key (TC) and vulnerability issue type ID (10024) from the Security Configuration in claude-md-security-config.md — not hardcoded values (§1.49)
assertion_results.evidence: In discovery-listing.md, all three JQL queries use 'project = TC AND issuetype = 10024'. The header explicitly states 'Project: TC' and 'Vulnerability issue type ID: 10024', confirming the values are sourced from configuration rather than hardcoded.

assertion_results.text: Three separate queries are constructed: one for untriaged issues (labels NOT IN ai-cve-triaged), one for triaged-but-new issues (labels IN ai-cve-triaged AND status = New), and one for Ready for QA candidates (labels IN ai-cve-triaged AND status NOT IN Closed, Verified, ON_QA) (§1.48)
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-listing.md contains three separate JQL queries: Query 1: 'project = TC AND issuetype = 10024 AND labels NOT IN (ai-cve-triaged) ORDER BY status ASC, created DESC'; Query 2: 'project = TC AND issuetype = 10024 AND labels IN (ai-cve-triaged) AND status = New ORDER BY created DESC'; Query 3: 'project = TC AND issuetype = 10024 AND labels IN (ai-cve-triaged) AND status NOT IN (Closed, Verified, 'ON_QA') ORDER BY created DESC'. All three match the required patterns exactly.

assertion_results.text: Results are grouped into three clearly separated lists: Untriaged issues, Triaged but still New issues, and Ready for QA issues
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-listing.md contains three clearly separated sections with horizontal rule dividers: 'Query 1: Untriaged Issues' (4 issues listed), 'Query 2: Triaged but still New' (1 issue listed), and 'Query 3: Ready for QA' (3 candidates evaluated, 1 qualifying). Each section has its own heading, JQL, and results.

assertion_results.text: Each listed issue shows: issue key, status, CVE ID (from labels), summary, and created date
assertion_results.evidence: All issues display all five fields. Examples from discovery-listing.md: 'TC-9003 | In Progress | CVE-2026-40455 | tokio - Race condition in task cancellation [rhtpa-2.2] | Created: 2026-06-05'; 'TC-9001 | New | CVE-2026-40112 | h2 - HTTP/2 rapid reset vulnerability [rhtpa-2.2] | Created: 2026-06-08'. The Ready for QA table also shows all fields: 'TC-9020 | Modified | CVE-2026-38901 | hyper - HTTP request smuggling [rhtpa-2.2] | 2026-05-15'. Excluded issues similarly show all fields in their table.

assertion_results.text: Status-aware handling: New issues (TC-9001, TC-9002, TC-9004) are presented for full triage, while the In Progress issue (TC-9003) triggers a warning about active work
assertion_results.evidence: In status-handling.md: TC-9001 (New): 'Proceed with full triage (default path). No warning needed.'; TC-9002 (New): 'Proceed with full triage (default path). No warning needed.'; TC-9004 (New): 'Proceed with full triage (default path). No warning needed.'; TC-9003 (In Progress): 'This issue is already in `In Progress`. It may be actively worked on.' with options to proceed or skip. The summary table confirms: TC-9003 -> 'Warn: may be actively worked on. Ask to proceed or skip'.

assertion_results.text: Ready for QA filtering: TC-9020 appears in Ready for QA (all linked Tasks Done/Closed), TC-9023 is excluded (TC-9025 still In Progress), TC-9026 is excluded (no Depend links)
assertion_results.evidence: In discovery-listing.md: 'TC-9020: ALL linked remediation Tasks completed (TC-9021 Done, TC-9022 Closed) -- Ready for QA'; 'TC-9023: TC-9025 still In Progress -- Excluded (remediation in progress)'; 'TC-9026: No linked Tasks with type "Depend" -- Excluded (no remediation to verify)'. The Ready for QA table lists only TC-9020, while the Excluded table lists TC-9023 and TC-9026 with their respective reasons.
